hypothesis: Fine-tuning pre-trained ML interatomic potentials (such as eSEN or eqV2) specifically on stress tensor data generated along DFT-converged SSNEB pathways—rather than on bulk equilibrium configurations alone—will disproportionately reduce stress prediction errors and thereby eliminate the symmetry-breaking artifacts currently observed during VASP restart steps, enabling fully ML-converged SSNEB pathways to achieve DFT-level accuracy in energy barriers without requiring any DFT refinement.
